skipLogic.description: Some questions trigger skips based on chosen option. skipTo: null means go to next question normally.

rules.reason: Low-saltiness users skip the deep hater question.
Non-hoopers skip the pickup tactics question.
Vibes-only fans skip the deep analytics question.

questions.title: Opening Tempo
questions.text: It's 2am on a weeknight and you're still up. Most likely reason:

options.text: Instagram dunk reels on autoplay. You came in for one clip, the sun is coming up.
West coast game is still in the third. You've been muttering "just two more minutes" for a full hour now.

You're in a thread war on r/NBA, tracing a timeline from LeBron's first "Decision" to KD joining the Warriors. The other side stopped replying an hour ago. You're still editing your seventh paragraph.

questions.title: Loyalty Exam
questions.text: July 4, 2016 — KD announces he's joining the Warriors. The push notification hits. Did your spine react?

options.text: Business is business. Players chase their own peak. I rolled over and went back to sleep — not my team, not my problem.
Felt a knot, then sighed and accepted it. Chasing rings isn't a crime. But "if you can't beat 'em, join 'em" doesn't read the same anymore.

Sat up in bed like you got hit by lightning. Changed your profile pic to a black candle in 90 seconds. Dropped three threads in a row: "73 wins wasn't enough," "a joiner for life," "league is cooked." Your hands were still shaking at 4am.

questions.title: Saltiness Strip
questions.text: Playoffs, Game 7, 1.2 seconds left. Your star gets undercut and the ref swallows the whistle. Your immediate physical reaction:

options.text: Deep breath. Refs are human. Phone off. Got work in the morning.
Six smiley faces and an ellipsis in the group chat. "Okay league, cool, cool." Passive-aggressive, but still dignified.

Fingers shaking, clip nine GIFs, draft a 1,500-word post titled "From the 2002 Lakers-Kings Game 6 to Tonight — A Chronology of Rigged Officiating." Views don't matter. The heart rate dropping back under 100 does.

questions.title: How Deep You Go (may skip to Q5)
questions.text: Last time you got into an online basketball fight that got you genuinely heated. Trigger was:

options.text: I don't fight online. I scroll past. Lifespan matters more than winning a thread.
Someone said something unhinged — like "Andrew Wiggins top-ten all-time" — I dropped two replies and peaced out.

They said "LeBron is worse than Curry." I went from the '03 draft to last night, posted six Basketball Reference screenshots, followed them until their account went private, and I still felt I wasn't finished.

questions.title: GOAT Debate Instinct
questions.text: Over dinner, someone raises a glass and declares, "Duncan is the consensus best power forward ever." Did the corner of your mouth twitch?

options.text: No. Duncan is great. That's consensus, right? What's the problem?
Yes. "Consensus according to whom?" rang in your head, Skip Bayless-style, and you almost snorted. You held it in for the sake of the room.

You put the fork down, took over the conversation, and delivered a twelve-minute tour from Malone's regular-season stats to KG's two-way case to Dirk's Finals run. By dessert the whole table was recalibrating their top-ten PFs.

questions.title: 58-Point Meme
questions.text: Forum post: "[Team] lost by 30 last night!" — your first reaction to the number is:

options.text: 30 is rough, that's a beating.
Only 30? Not in the 58-tier. Not a historic one.
"0.52 of a 58. Not even passing. In January 2009 Phoenix put up a full 58 on CP3 in his own building. THAT is the measuring stick. 30 doesn't chart."

questions.title: Arena Proximity
questions.text: A friend texts you a ticket to tonight's marquee game. Your realistic distance from "live NBA experience" is:

options.text: The closest I've come to a real arena is spotting someone in the front row holding the wrong foam finger.
Been a few times. First I pick my angles, take photos, post a story to prove I'm not a TV-only fan. The actual game kind of blurs.
Reply: what row, what seat, can we stalk the tunnel, what's the arena wifi, and where's the closest craft beer bar. You go to games like you're producing an event.

questions.title: A Curry Moment
questions.text: You're mid-bite at dinner, and Steph drills a deep logo three for the win. Your body reacts before your brain. You:

options.text: Mutter "damn," then finish chewing. Beautiful shot. Food doesn't wait.
Screenshot it for your story. Caption was drafted mid-air: "this shouldn't go in."

Chopsticks slap the table, phone is already up, and you @ the three people who called him cooked last week, attached slow-mo GIF with the caption: "Others call it a heave — when he takes it, it's a logo shot."

questions.title: Tank Stress Test
questions.text: Your team starts 2-17. The subreddit's sticky has already rebranded from "Playoff Push" to "Protect the Lottery Pick." Your mental state:

options.text: Quietly switch over to a contender's stream. Come back when the rebuild is over.
Start eating draft scouting reports for breakfast. Research the next potential franchise savior. Painful, but there's direction.
The more losses, the hype. Post "winning this one actually cost us half our future." Cheer up the group chat post-loss with "keep it up fellas, that top pick is incoming."

questions.title: Pickup Identity
questions.text: The park is one short. Someone yells "yo — you, come in!" Gap between your actual level and your self-image:

options.text: I'm the "you hoop, you go" guy. Once I'm actually in, I retie shoelaces for five minutes, then stand in the corner as a human cone.
I can run the floor, rotate on defense, shoot open ones. Makes are another story, but I'm not the worst out there.
I warm up with step-back threes, call for the ball first possession, self-declared "blacktop Kobe" — shoot at roughly the same percentage the '18 Rockets hit from three in Game 7 of the WCF.

questions.title: Pickup Tactics (may skip to Q12)
questions.text: Park game, your squad asks "what do we run?" You:

options.text: Tactics? Whoever gets the ball, shoots it.
Just cut off the ball, shoot when open, don't over-dribble.
Grab a piece of chalk, sketch a set on the floor, walk them through sideline inbound, then weakside cut. Your squad thinks you're annoying — but the first play actually runs.

questions.title: Your Star Joins a Rival
questions.text: Your favorite player signs with the one team you actively hate. First involuntary body move:

options.text: Whatever. I follow the game, not the laundry. Jerseys are jerseys.
Still a fan of him as a person — but won't lift a finger to defend that team. Feels like a one-sided divorce with lingering feelings.

Morning: "If you can't beat 'em, join 'em is trash." Afternoon: new profile pic, new wallpaper, cart full of merch, Twitter bio updated. Your mouth says one thing, your body does the other — body wins, obviously.

questions.title: Jordan vs. LeBron
questions.text: Someone at the coffee machine loudly says "Jordan is better than LeBron" (or the reverse), clearly baiting. Do you bite?

options.text: Pretend I didn't hear. My 9:30am blood pressure isn't worth this conversation.
I have a full counter-argument queued up, but a look around the office convinces me to shut up. Not worth exposing the BBIQ to coworkers.

Slowly put the coffee cup down. Open the Notes app. You have a 47-page document titled "Comparative Legacy Analysis" — ring weighted, FMVP voting breakdown, era-adjusted PER. Proceed like a congressional hearing.

questions.title: The J.R. Smith Anniversary
questions.text: It's June. Someone inevitably re-posts J.R. Smith running out the clock in Game 1 of the 2018 Finals. You:

options.text: What pass? Doesn't ring a bell.
Chest tightens. You know the meme, you just don't want to watch it one more time.

You post your annual "Never Forget" thread on time, with the GIF, the "he thought they were up" caption, and commemorative emojis. It's been eight straight years. It's basically a holiday for you now.

questions.title: 35 Seconds, 13 Points
questions.text: You're drunk at a bar. Somebody shouts "35 SECONDS, 13 POINTS!" Your first reaction:

options.text: Is this some kind of mental math problem?
T-Mac! Vs. Spurs! I know this one!

December 9, 2004. Toyota Center. Rockets trailing the Spurs 84-81. In the final 35 seconds T-Mac drops 13, capped with a four-point play. You walk the bar through it possession by possession. The next song is playing. You're still not done.

questions.title: Drake Curse Litmus
questions.text: At dinner, someone joke-references the Drake Curse and pokes you with "is r/NBA still doing the 'Basketball Reasons' copypasta?" You:

options.text: Smile politely. That meme is ancient. Not worth the energy.
Appreciate it like a museum piece, laugh, move on. Certified Basketball Twitter fossil.

Cut the table off. Walk through the entire Drake Curse timeline — Alabama, KY, Arsenal, Kendrick — plus the r/NBA "Basketball Reasons" lineage all the way to the mod sticker wars. Turn dinner into a meme studies seminar.

questions.title: NBA Internet Niche
questions.text: Your team's name is trending. Your position in the r/NBA / NBA Twitter / basketball Discord ecosystem is:

options.text: Lurker. Scroll in, scroll out. Still using the default egg avatar.
Your threads start with "Calm discussion" and stay calm for at least three paragraphs.

Regulars see your username and know court is in session. Your hater meme folder and frame-by-frame GIFs outgun the mods. Before the other side finishes typing, your receipts have scrolled to page two.

questions.title: Weapon of Choice
questions.text: Group chat is eight pages deep in "is ___ really a superstar?" Your go-to weapon:

options.text: Just a "he's a superstar? lmaooo." No logic, pure tone.
Averages, TS%, last five games, a sarcastic "win a ring first" — loud but not hardcore.
TS%, BPM, VORP, On/Off net rating, clutch-time splits — all at once. Thread becomes a thesis defense. Not open to rebuttal, only open to more data.

questions.title: Deep-Dive Analytics (may skip to Q20)
questions.text: A comment drops: "That 40-piece was padded." You:

options.text: Dropping 40 is impressive, padded or not. Respect.
Some of it is garbage, sure, but it's not all padding. Depends on opponent strength.
Immediately pull the garbage-time share, opponent defensive rating rank, and his usage curve. Follow up with: "define 'padded' — are we measuring it in rings, efficiency, or 58s?"

questions.title: Fan Conversion
questions.text: You dragged a non-fan friend to watch the Finals with you. End result:

options.text: All they remembered was the halftime ad and the DoorDash order. They now want to watch even less basketball.
They learned two star names. Might be willing to sit through another Finals game next year.
They now have their own team. They've learned to say "that was a foul," "this shot is heat-check," "the future is ours," and they started trash-talking YOUR team. You created a monster.

questions.title: Consumption Profile
questions.text: Black Friday dumps an entire page of "jerseys, sneakers, trading cards 50% off tonight only" in your face. Your spend personality is:

options.text: Close tab. My fandom doesn't need a receipt.
Compare prices carefully, buy one thing. Consider it this season's membership dues.

Enter "NBA luxury tax" mode. Say you're being responsible, then add both the limited edition and the autographed one to cart. When the box arrives: "this isn't impulse, this is investing in the faith."

questions.title: Watch Party Role
questions.text: A local fan meetup runs a watch party. Rule sheet: "wear your jersey, don't leave early if we lose." You usually:

options.text: First I've heard of this existing as a thing.
Been a few times. I handle clapping, hype noises, group photos.
I'm the person who organizes it. Projector, banner, chants, raffles, drinks — all coordinated from my phone. I've turned watch parties into my own little Finals operation.

questions.title: Final Loyalty Test
questions.text: Someone asks you sincerely: "Your team is a tire fire. Why aren't you switching?" You answer most like:

options.text: "I root for winners, not the process." (Immediately opens a futures page to check this year's title odds.)
"Not switching. But I reserve the right to roast the front office daily." For you, loyalty and venting are fully compatible.
"Because love." Then you open the rebuild thread and call "this is our year" like you're previewing a lottery drawing — twelfth straight year you've said it.

questions.title: Phone Unlock Test
questions.text: Hand someone your unlocked phone for 10 seconds. What's the first thing they'd notice?

options.text: A score-tracking app, barely. Nothing else suggests basketball is in your life at all.
Player wallpaper, a few basketball accounts followed, some highlight saves — but nowhere near obsessed.
Full schedule in your calendar, fan group chat pinned, album full of screenshots and GIFs, and trade rumors filed in their own folder. Your phone looks like a bootleg front office.

questions.title: Finale — Playoff Elimination Game
questions.text: Today is your team's elimination game. From afternoon onward, you're most like:

options.text: Work, dinner, life — normal. I'll catch it when it tips. One game can't disrupt the day.
Schedule cleared early, channel set, snacks queued. Palms sweat in the clutch, but I'll never admit I'm nervous.

Jersey on two hours early, snacks and drinks arranged, internet stress-tested, notifications silenced. Treating this like you're staging a whole pregame ceremony for yourself. Three celebration posts drafted if we win. Tomorrow's PTO already requested if we lose.

Cracked a beer before tip. First quarter, still analyzing rotations. Second quarter, fighting with the live chat. Fourth quarter, on my feet yelling at the ref in my worst indoor voice. Woke up the next day remembering none of it — just that I definitely cursed someone out.
